\begin{document}

\title{Domed Monuments Cluster at Babylonian B\={e}ru Harmonics:\\
A Longitude Enrichment Test on the UNESCO World Heritage List}

\author{Seth Tenenbaum\thanks{ORCID: \href{https://orcid.org/0009-0008-5797-2498}{0009-0008-5797-2498}.
Correspondence: seth@fourthtemple.com}\\
Fourth Temple Research, USA}

\maketitle

\abstract[ABSTRACT]{
Domed monuments in the UNESCO World Heritage Cultural and Mixed corpus
cluster at harmonics of the Babylonian b\={e}ru (30\textdegree{},
MUL.APIN, c.~1000~BCE) when longitudes are referenced to Mount Gerizim
(\GerizimLon\textdegree{}E), a historically attested terrestrial meridian.
Among $N=\NcircTotal$ dome/spherical sites, \NcircTierApp{} fall within
one parasang ($\sim\AppThreshKm{}$~km) of a harmonic node,
$\circEnrichApp\times$ above chance
($p=\pCircAppFisher$, \pCircAppFisherSig{}; survives Bonferroni at
$k=\NtotalTests$).
Enrichment within the broader $\sim\ApThreshKm{}$~km window also reaches the
primary Bonferroni bound ($p_{\mathrm{adj}}=\pAdjTestTwo$, $k=\BonfK$),
and cluster asymmetry independently reaches significance
($p_{\mathrm{adj}}=\pAdjTestThree$).
The full Cultural/Mixed corpus ($N=\NclusterTotal$) shows significant
global enrichment ($p_{\mathrm{adj}}=\pAdjTestOne$, \pAdjTestOneSig{}).
The anchor is historically motivated; the pipeline is fully reproducible.
}

\bigskip

\noindent\textbf{Keywords:}
longitude periodicity, spatial statistics, Levant, UNESCO World Heritage,
Babylonian b\={e}ru, ancient metrology, domed architecture, archaeoastronomy,
monument distribution, MUL.APIN

\bigskip
